title: Rule-Test-Application-BusinessAction
description: JSON schema for creating or updating a Pega Business Action (Rule-Test-Application-BusinessAction) via create-rule / update-rule. Only authored and identity fields are included; system-managed and versioning fields are omitted.

pyClassName.description: Applies-to class (case type work class). Example: MyOrg-MyApp-Work-MyCaseType

pyRuleSet.description: Ruleset name for the Business Action rule.

pyPurpose.description: Identity key (PascalCase, no spaces). Combined with pyClassName forms unique rule key. Max 64 characters. Example: CreateHomeloanCase

pyLabel.description: Human-readable label displayed in App Studio. Max 64 characters. Example: Create Home loan Case

pyKeywordDescription.description: BDD-style description for the business action. Example: User Creates new Home loan Case

pyKeywordType.description: BDD keyword type. Always WHEN for Business Actions.

pyBusinessActionDecision.description: Describes the decision/outcome after this action completes. Example: If Reviewer marks Application status as Application complete, then Property valuation child case gets created.

pyBusinessActionValidation.description: Validation notes for this business action.

pxCaseName.description: Display name of the case type. Example: Home loan

pyIsAssertion.description: Whether this Business Action is an assertion (validation-only). Always false for standard Business Actions.

pySkipValidations.description: Whether to skip form validations during execution.

pyIsBASelected.description: Whether this Business Action is selected for test generation.

pyStepPageReference.description: Step page reference for execution context. Typically RH_1.

pyInputParameters.description: Input parameters for the Business Action. These provide test data and execution context.

pyOutputParameters.description: Root-level output parameters captured from the overall Business Action execution. Exactly ONE entry for CreateCase BAs: descriptive name (e.g., HomeLoanCaseID) with pyMapOutputFrom=Response. NEVER put generic CaseID or pyParameterValue here — those belong at step level only.

pyTestSteps.description: Ordered list of automation steps. Steps execute sequentially.

pyPlaywrightMethod.description: Function call signature for UI automation. Example: FunctionName(page, browser, context, Param1, Param2);

pyPlaywrightScript.description: Full async TypeScript function body for Playwright UI automation.

pyParameterName.description: Parameter name. Examples: CaseID, First_name, ValidationFails

pyParameterValue.description: Default test data value. Examples: John, 250000, False

CreateCaseStep.description: Creates a new case instance and captures the CaseID.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: User creates new Home loan case

pyActionParameters.description: Parameters passed to the DX API for case creation.

PerformAssignmentStep.description: Fills and submits an assignment form via DX API.

pyAssignmentName.description: Assignment name matching the flow action label. Example: Living situation

pyExecutionContext.description: Class context for assignment execution. Same as rule's pyClassName.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: User submits Living situation form

pyForm.description: Maps input parameters to form fields for API submission. Standard fields use FormMapping; single-reference fields use ReferenceFormMapping; multi-reference fields use MultiReferenceFormMapping.

pyActionParameters.description: Parameters passed to the DX API for assignment submission.

AssertStageStep.description: Verifies the case has reached an expected stage.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: Verify Case Stage

PerformActionStep.description: Executes a case-wide action (not tied to a specific assignment). Actions are accessed via the case actions menu (e.g., Change stage, Reopen, Edit details).

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable description. Example: User submits Change stage Action

pyForm.description: Maps input parameters to form fields. Checkboxes are NEVER included in pyForm. Single-reference fields use ReferenceFormMapping; multi-reference fields use MultiReferenceFormMapping.

pyActionParameters.description: Parameters: CaseID (Input) and Action Name (Constant, e.g., pyChangeStage, pyReopen, pyUpdateCaseDetails).

UserContextStep.description: Sets user login context (URL and Persona) for test execution.

pyExecutionContext.description: Class context. Typically Work- for foundation Business Actions.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: User logins to system

GetCaseStep.description: Opens/retrieves an existing case by CaseID.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: Navigate to case

AssertStatusStep.description: Asserts the case has reached an expected status.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: Verify case status is Resolved-Completed

AssertFieldStep.description: Asserts a specific field value on a case view.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: Verify field value matches expected

AssertErrorStep.description: Asserts a validation error message is displayed.

pyStepDescription.description: Human-readable step description. Example: Verify validation error is shown

FormMapping.description: Standard form field mapping for non-reference fields (TextInput, Dropdown, RadioButtons, etc.).

pyParameterName.description: Field ID from view rule pyFieldReference (e.g., CurrentAddress, FirstName)

pyParameterValue.description: Input parameter label that provides the value (e.g., What's your current address?)

pyMapRequestFieldFrom.description: Source of field value. Always Input for parameter-driven fields.

pyParameterType.description: Optional type hint for the parameter value. Example: String

ReferenceFormMapping.description: Form field mapping for single-reference fields (singleselect autocomplete). Used when a view field has referenceFields with mode=singleselect. Does NOT use pyMapRequestFieldFrom or pyParameterValue at root — uses pyTestReferenceField instead.

pyParameterName.description: The referenceID from the view's ObjectReference field (Step 2). Example: FeatureCase, Sprint, ProductVersion, BugFoundInRelease

pyParameterType.description: Always Reference for singleselect reference fields.

pyTestReferenceField.description: Sub-parameters mapping selectionKey to the input parameter that provides the value.

MultiReferenceFormMapping.description: Form field mapping for multi-reference fields (multiselect table). Used when a view field has referenceFields with mode=multiselect. Uses pyTestMultiReferenceList containing entries with pyTestReferenceField.

pyParameterName.description: The referenceID from the view's ObjectReference field (Step 2). Example: DuplicateBugList, ExistingTestAssets

pyParameterType.description: Always Multi-Reference (with hyphen) for multiselect reference fields.

pyTestMultiReferenceList.description: List of multi-reference selection entries. Each entry contains a pyTestReferenceField array.

MultiReferenceEntry.description: An entry in pyTestMultiReferenceList containing the reference field selection parameters.

pyTestReferenceField.description: Selection key parameters. Uses pyMapActionParameterFrom: Constant.

MultiReferenceFieldParameter.description: A sub-parameter inside multi-reference pyTestReferenceField. Maps field with Constant source and relevant test data value.

pyParameterName.description: The selectionKey or additional field from the view's ObjectReference field. Typically pyID.

pyParameterValue.description: Relevant test data value for this row selection. Examples: BUG-101, FEAT-42, Sprint 5. Must not be empty.

pyMapActionParameterFrom.description: Always Constant for multi-reference table row selections.

ReferenceFieldParameter.description: A sub-parameter inside single-reference pyTestReferenceField. Maps selectionKey to the input parameter providing the value.

pyParameterName.description: The selectionKey from the view's ObjectReference field. Typically pyID.

pyParameterValue.description: The input parameter name that provides the reference value. Matches the referenceFields label. Example: Feature, Sprint, Found in release

pyMapActionParameterFrom.description: How the value is resolved. Input = from pyInputParameters (most common for singleselect references).

pyParameterName.description: Action parameter name. Common values: CaseID, Assignment, CaseType, ProcessID, ValidationFails

pyParameterValue.description: Parameter value or reference. For Constant: literal value. For Input: input parameter name.

pyMapActionParameterFrom.description: How the value is resolved. Constant = literal value. Input = from pyInputParameters.

OutputParameter.description: Step-level output parameter captured from a step's API response. Uses pyParameterValue to specify JSON response path. The pyParameterName must be a descriptive case-type-specific name (e.g., HomeLoanCaseID) — the SAME name used at root-level. NEVER use generic CaseID.

pyParameterName.description: Descriptive case-type-specific name matching root-level. Example: HomeLoanCaseID. NEVER generic CaseID.

pyParameterValue.description: Response path to extract value from. Always: data$caseInfo$ID

RootOutputParameter.description: Root-level output parameter exported by the Business Action to the Test Case. Uses pyMapOutputFrom (always Response). The pyParameterName must be a descriptive case-type-specific name (e.g., HomeLoanCaseID) — the SAME name used at step-level. NEVER use generic CaseID. NEVER use pyParameterValue.

pyParameterName.description: Descriptive case-type-specific name matching step-level. Example: HomeLoanCaseID. NEVER generic CaseID.

pyMapOutputFrom.description: Source type. Always Response for root-level outputs.
